content: Autonomous curiosity-driven agents using ICM mechanisms can self-tune their uncertainty-based memory sampling thresholds by repurposing their intrinsic curiosity prediction error as a dynamic selection signal. This creates a closed-loop system where the agent’s internal drive to explore automatically adapts which memory samples it retains and reuses for active learning, eliminating the need for hand-tuned static uncertainty thresholds.
source_topics: Uncertainty-based memory sample selection for deep active agents
